concepts.title: The Idea: Discard Success, Commit Failure
Privacy: Allowlist Capture & the Write-Only Wall
The Control Protocol: Talking to the Fleet
Concurrency: Hundreds of Requests at Once

images.alt: A stream of execution events: success evaporates, failure is captured as a checkpoint crystal
images.caption: Every request records everything. Success evaporates; only failure is kept.
images.prompt: A clean, modern editorial illustration for a developer book cover, cyan-on-deep-navy blueprint/schematic aesthetic. Central motif: a stream of small glowing execution events flowing left-to-right into a valve; on success the stream evaporates into faint dissipating particles, on failure a single crisp checkpoint crystal is captured and outlined like a technical schematic diagram. Faint drafting grid in the background, thin cyan technical linework, a few monospaced tick-marks like an engineering drawing. No readable text baked in. Mood: precise, industrial, confident. Target size ~1600x1000px, 16:10 landscape; the subject centred with generous safe margins, letterboxed into the box.

images.alt: Two tracks: on success the buffer vanishes, on failure it is preserved as an artifact file
images.caption: Same recording, two fates — decided only at the end of the request.
images.prompt: A minimalist editorial diagram-illustration in a cyan-on-navy blueprint style: two parallel tracks. Top track labelled by shape only (no text) shows an in-memory buffer filling with small event marks and then vanishing into nothing at the end — success. Bottom track shows the same buffer filling, then at the end a bright cyan artifact file icon is hard-linked out and preserved — failure. Thin technical linework, drafting-grid background, schematic arrows. No readable words baked in. Target size ~1280x720px, 16:9 landscape, subject centred with safe margins, letterboxed.

images.alt: A storm of identical failures funneled through a token bucket; only a few pass, the rest are counted
images.caption: A correlated storm: one checkpoint is kept per signature per minute, the rest are counted, not lost.
images.prompt: A cyan-on-navy blueprint-style editorial illustration of a failure storm: hundreds of identical small error glyphs raining down toward a funnel/token-bucket that lets only a few drops through into a single labelled checkpoint, while a counter tallies the suppressed rest. Thin technical schematic linework, drafting grid, one bright cyan accent on the drops that pass. No readable text baked in. Target size ~1280x720px, 16:9 landscape, subject centred with safe margins, letterboxed.

images.alt: An agent inside a customer boundary opening a single outbound connection to a control plane; a narrow capability envelope gates what the platform can ask
images.caption: One outbound connection, a narrow envelope. The platform can make the agent do less — never more.
images.prompt: A cyan-on-navy blueprint-style editorial illustration: inside a walled customer boundary, a single agent opens one thin outbound arrow up to a distant "control plane" node — no arrow ever points back in. Between them sits a narrow keyhole/valve labelled by shape only, letting only small "verdict" tokens pass downward while blocking larger "command" shapes. Thin technical schematic linework, drafting grid, one bright cyan accent on the outbound line, orange on the blocked commands. No readable text baked in. Target size ~1280x720px, 16:9 landscape, subject centred with safe margins, letterboxed.

summary: A hands-on guide for developers wiring LinkLog into their Python services — capture failures, keep the success path invisible, and hand the sidecar a forensic checkpoint.
title: Integrating LinkLog: The Python SDK Field Guide
topic: integrating the LinkLog failure-intelligence platform into a Python application using the llog Python SDK
